title: Coinbase goes public, direct listings vs. IPOs, portfolio management, unions & more with Bestie Guestie Brad Gerstner
description:

Show Notes:

0:00 Bestie intro & crowning a new SPAC king

3:01 Coinbase's direct listing, comparing vehicles to go public & lockup periods, Sacks on Coinbase vs. NYT

12:06 David Sacks on running for governor

15:21 Brad & the besties react to the crazy Q1 in the markets, a16z's savvy buying moves with Coinbase, issues with short-term behavioral lock-in

32:37 Top insights from Bezos' letter to shareholders, Amazon employees reject the union in Alabama

50:27 Reacting to Drew Holden's recent thread on media double standards in covering each party

54:28 Federalism's benefits throughout COVID, State-by-state results reveal lockdowns didn't work, vaccine incentives and PR due to J&J decision

1:04:20 Degradation of faith in institutions accelerated by COVID

1:09:26 Republican Senator Josh Hawley calls for big tech antitrust reform, bypassing institutions for progress

analysis.summary: The episode covers various topics including Coinbase's direct listing, portfolio management, unions, and the degradation of faith in institutions. The hosts also discuss the benefits of federalism and the issues with short-term behavioral lock-in. Additionally, they react to recent news and tweets from notable figures.

takeaways.text: Direct listings can be a viable alternative to traditional IPOs
Short-term behavioral lock-in can be a major issue in investing
Federalism has shown benefits during the COVID-19 pandemic
Faith in institutions has been degraded due to the pandemic
Big tech antitrust reform is being called for by some politicians
Lockdowns did not have a significant impact on COVID-19 cases
Vaccine incentives and PR have been affected by the J&J decision
Media double standards have been observed in covering different parties

sentiment.notes: The hosts discuss a wide range of topics, some with a positive tone and others with a more critical tone, resulting in a mixed sentiment overall.

notable_moments.description: David Sacks discusses running for governor
The hosts react to Bezos' letter to shareholders
The hosts discuss the benefits of federalism during the COVID-19 pandemic
